text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. One critical aspect is responsive design, which ensures the app looks and works well on different screen sizes. Developers must choose between native and cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built for a specific operating system, like iOS or Android, offering better performance and access to device features. Cross-platform tools, such as React Native, allow a single codebase to run on multiple systems, saving time and money. After development, apps are submitted to app stores like Apple’s App Store or Google Play, where they are reviewed before release. Performance is vital—users expect fast loading times, smooth navigation, and minimal battery drain. A well-optimized app leads to higher user satisfaction and retention.
